items.title: Teenager arrested after remains found during search for missing 13-year-old girl

children.content: Detectives are investigating after human remains were found during a search for a missing teenage girl in Victoria's west.

gallery.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

items.title: Telstra's returning CEO Vicki Brady 'deeply sorry' for national outage

children.content: Telstra chief executive Vicki Brady apologises to the company's customers, saying the telco let them down and that investigations into the cause of Wednesday's Telstra outage are continuing.

image.altText: Vicky Brady speaks for the first time since Telstra outage. She has short brown hair and brown eyes. Wears a navy suit.

items.title: First native bird found with H5 bird flu, marking 'devastating' new reality

children.content: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

image.altText: A greater crested tern on South Africa's western cape.

items.title: Derryn Hinch remembered as 'tough old bugger', 'crusader for justice'

children.content: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

articles.title: Derryn Hinch, 'human headline' and broadcaster, dies aged 82

children.content: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate, and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders that earned him several stints in jail.

image.altText: Hinch, dressed in a red scarf, talks to reporters outside Parliament House in Canberra

gallery.altText: A man smiles while posing in front of a long coastline and blue sky.
Media personality and former politician Derryn Hinch, pictured in his Melbourne office in 1985 in front of a typewriter.
Derryn Hinch wears a colourful tie and black suit in Parliament.

thumbnail.altText: A man smiles while posing in front of a long coastline and blue sky.
Media personality and former politician Derryn Hinch, pictured in his Melbourne office in 1985 in front of a typewriter.
Derryn Hinch wears a colourful tie and black suit in Parliament.

items.title: 'Incredible musician': Cold Chisel saxophonist Andy Bickers dies from cancer

children.content: Cold Chisel saxophone player Andy Bickers has died after "hard fought battle with cancer", the band has announced.

image.altText: A man playing a saxophone on a stage

items.title: Secret Service reportedly urged Trump to leave Türkiye on old Air Force One

children.content: Officials reportedly urged the US president to swap out his Qatari-donated luxury jet as a security precaution due to renewed hostilities with Iran.

image.altText: Donald Trump walks down the stairs off a large jet plane at night, two men follow him on the stairs

items.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket recovery

children.content: China has successfully tested an experimental rocket retrieval system, state media reported, in the hope of breaking US dominance in reusable rockets.

image.altText: Long March-10B rocket airborne above an offshore recovery platform.

gallery.altText: Long March-10B rocket airborne above an offshore recovery platform.

thumbnail.altText: Long March-10B rocket airborne above an offshore recovery platform.

items.title: Economist Justin Wolfers on the 'absurdity' of Trump's America

children.content: Former Reserve Bank economist Justin Wolfers says Australia urgently needs to consider the role of money in its politics while examining how US President Donald Trump profits from his position.

image.altText: A man smiles at the camera.

items.title: Pies win a thriller against Kangaroos as veteran faces MRO scrutiny

children.content: Collingwood wins a thriller against North Melbourne after a last ditch kick from the Roos hits the turf.

image.altText: GettyImages-2284848594

items.title: 'Not gonna talk about that': Luai dodges press conference after another Tigers loss

children.content: The Tigers lose 32-6 to the Warriors at Campbelltown Stadium, with Jarome Luai skipping the post-match press conference after a week of reports about his future with the team.

image.altText: Jarome Luai runs with the ball during a Wests Tigers NRL game.

items.title: Bayeux Tapestry returns to Britain in secret night-time operation

children.content: The Bayeux Tapestry has arrived in Britain for the first time in nearly 1,000 years, travelling under police escort during a meticulously choreographed journey from France.

image.altText: A woman points a cloth tapestry

items.title: Australia's most played song on radio this week draws AI criticisms

children.content: A cover of the Madonna hit Like a Prayer is at the top of the charts but criticisms have been levied at the artist behind the cover, Josh Fawaz, over his suspected use of artificial intelligence.

gallery.altText: Madonna press pic, long blonde hair, silver jacket, blue pants, bare legs

thumbnail.altText: Madonna press pic, long blonde hair, silver jacket, blue pants, bare legs

items.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up art museum in Thailand

children.content: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

image.altText: An artist impression of a modern, triangular-shaped building, surrounded by a pond.

items.title: Australia's huntsman fastest spider on record, new study suggests

children.content: A species of huntsman spider found in Queensland is the fastest mover out of more than 250 spider species analysed in a new study.

image.altText: A brown spider balancing on three green twigs framed like a window at night.

items.title: This Place: Boonoo Moodla

children.content: Boonoo Moodla, Billy Lights Point, has been a place of gathering and fishing for the Barngarla people for thousands of years.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.

image.altText: This Place: Boonoo Moodla

items.title: Sydney woman accused of illegal voting facing jury trial in US

children.content: A Sydney woman will face trial in the US state of Louisiana after being accused of falsely claiming to be a US citizen and voting illegally in two federal elections.

image.altText: A woman with brown hair smiles while wearing a dotty black shirt.

items.title: Pedestrians 'lucky to be alive' after truck loses load on highway

children.content: Footage shows two 15-tonne concrete slabs colliding with parked cars when the incident happened north of Adelaide.

image.altText: Large concrete slabs fall from a moving truck on a motorway

items.title: Cattle farmer plants 14,000 trees to help slow flow of flood water

children.content: Jeff Imeson has planted around 14,000 trees on his property near Nimbin in a bid to help slow the flow of future floods.

gallery.altText: Farmer inspects a baby tree planted two years ago to help stabilise a creek bank in the Norther Rivers
Bird eye view shot of farm land showing lots of new plantings on Jeff Imeson's property in the Northern Rivers
A man and a woman look out over a creek bank at new tree planting to help reduce flood damage

thumbnail.altText: Farmer inspects a baby tree planted two years ago to help stabilise a creek bank in the Norther Rivers
Bird eye view shot of farm land showing lots of new plantings on Jeff Imeson's property in the Northern Rivers
A man and a woman look out over a creek bank at new tree planting to help reduce flood damage

items.title: Not only is the Iran war not over, it is at a dangerous inflection point

children.content: Not only is the war not over, it is once again at a dangerous inflection point, as the US and Iran revert to military options to deal with differences over their interim peace agreement.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks in front of a US flag.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: The Rolling Stones prove 'This never gets old!' with new album

children.content: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

image.altText: The Rolling Stones's Mick Jagger, Keith Richards and Ronnie Wood pose and smile together.

items.title: The lowdown on lush native lawns that only need mowing once a year

children.content: If mowing the lawn is getting you down, it might be time to ditch your exotic buffalo, couch or kikuyu grass and switch to a native alternative.

image.altText: A close up of a person in work boots walking across a lush green native lawn.

items.title: Corruption watchdog's university probe ends in tears

children.content: Former University of Wollongong governance officer Alyssa White became emotional as she explained to an ICAC inquiry why she repeatedly intervened to secure jobs for friends and former colleagues.

image.altText: Woman sitting in a chair.

items.title: Eight charged over alleged plot to kill Trump, Musk and Netanyahu at UFC fight

children.content: Eight men have been accused of planning a drone and shooting attack that aimed to kill the US president and SpaceX founder at the UFC Freedom 250 event held at the White House in June.

gallery.altText: A canopy over a UFC ring in the dark sky with the White House in the background

thumbnail.altText: A canopy over a UFC ring in the dark sky with the White House in the background

items.title: Family of missing Toowoomba woman fears the worst

children.content: Police release CCTV footage and switch search focus as the family of young mother Jana Armstrong, who went missing in Toowoomba, fears the worst.

image.altText: woman standing in hallway

items.title: Australian-first CO2-powered battery planned for coal town

children.content: The Victorian government unveils a plan to build a compressed CO2 battery storage system in the heart of the state's coal country.

image.altText: Graphic of a green paddock with rolling hills in the background, with a white curved shed in the centre of the image.

items.title: X under fire for 'shocking disrespect' of antisemitism royal commission

children.content: Social media giants, including Elon Musk's X Corp, have come under fire with the royal commission on antisemitism expressing frustration at platforms' refusal to engage.

image.altText: Elon Musk pouts his lips in a close up portrait

items.title: Musician loses discrimination fight against orchestra over Gaza comments

children.content: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, 39, bearded, in a suit, including black tie, walks outside a court.

items.title: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win World Cup in any fashion

children.content: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

articles.title: Quick hits: It's the Mbappé show again, as England defender cops ban

children.content: An England defender pays the price for a terrible red-card tackle, and it's the Mbappé show in Boston as France advances. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Chemsdine Chalbi, Kylian Mbappe, Jarell Quansah

articles.title: France's superstars step up to beat Morocco and reach World Cup semis

children.content: Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé deliver France a comprehensive 2-0 win over Morocco to send Les Bleus into the World Cup semifinals.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappe clenches his fists and smiles while looking at the camera

gallery.altText: Kylian Mbappe has his arms outstretched and a triumphant look on his face after scoring

thumbnail.altText: Kylian Mbappe has his arms outstretched and a triumphant look on his face after scoring

items.title: Brisbane Airport flight paths altered following noise review

children.content: People living in areas such as Redcliffe, southern Moreton Bay, Bribie Island and Logan are set to benefit the most, but there will be no change to the flight path over Bulimba.

image.altText: A qantas plane taking off into a clear blue sky with a city skyline at the bottom in the distance

items.title: Charlie Kirk's family requests evidence against accused killer be released

children.content: The family of conservative activist Charlie Kirk has pleaded with a judge to allow more public access to evidence presented against the man accused of the assassination.

image.altText: A rear view of a Robinson wearing a white button-up shirt sitting in a courtroom.

items.title: Looking back at Bonnie Tyler's impact on pop culture

children.content: Tyler's music continuously found newer, younger audiences thanks to it soundtracking memorable scenes in movies throughout the decades.

gallery.altText: An animation of Shrek 2 character Fairy Godmother singing on stage in a red dress

thumbnail.altText: An animation of Shrek 2 character Fairy Godmother singing on stage in a red dress

items.title: Big Bash to play historic season-opener in India

children.content: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

image.altText: Nick Hobson of the Scorchers and Aaron Hardie of the Scorchers pose with the BBL trophy

items.title: The 'stunt' that could cost UK far right leader Nigel Farage his political career

children.content: Nigel Farage is facing intensifying scrutiny over his finances and millions of dollars' worth of donations. Here's how he got here.

image.altText: A man in a dark suit and pale lavender tie smiles while holding a beer outside a British pub.

items.title: The meteorite at the centre of a 'culture war' between the UK and Australia

children.content: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

image.altText: A meteorite with a tail of flame burning behind it hits the Earth with a purple sky above.

articles.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

articles.title: Indigenous-led whale tour shares thousands of years of observations and knowledge

children.content: A new Indigenous-led whale-watching cruise is aiming to give an intimate insight into First Nations people's deep historical connection with the majestic mammals.

image.altText: Two large whales breach over the water and are captured mid air.

gallery.altText: A meteorite travels through deep space lit up from the inside.

thumbnail.altText: A meteorite travels through deep space lit up from the inside.
